chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating whether an entity is currently active or inactive.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A registered individual or entity on the platform who has made at least one purchase and is identified by a unique Customer ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
CUSTOMER
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID | **Key Attributes**:
- Valid email address (required for login) | **Key Attributes**:
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Key Attributes**:
- Geographic region for tax calculation | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | # Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description*

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A line item in a sales order representing a single product within that order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) |  | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A single line item in a Sales Order representing a specific product, the quantity ordered, and the unit price for that item.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
ORDER LINE ITEM
**Definition**: A single line within a Sales Order specifying a Product, quantity, and unit price at time of purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A self-referencing foreign key column in the TB_CATEGORY table that stores the ID of the parent category, enabling a hierarchical category structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
| PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES |

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Payment is a financial transaction record that settles a Sales Order, identified by a unique PAYMENT_ID and including attributes such as the payment amount.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Payment amount | **Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships | **Business Rules**:
- Refunds create new payment records with negative amounts | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: Sales Order Header is the header record for each customer sales order, identified by ORDER_ID, with CUST_ID linking to the Customer.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
| TOTAL_AMT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Total order value | NO | | - CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the business entity representing a dispatched package for a Sales Order, identified by a unique shipment ID, associated with a source warehouse, and potentially carrying a tracking number.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Tracking number | **Key Attributes**:
- Destination Customer Address | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | | WAREHOUSE_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | Source warehouse | YES | | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID column represents the unique identifier (primary key) of a product in the TB_PRODUCT table, used to identify products in orders and line items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: Unit price is the price charged per unit of a product or item, typically expressed in a base currency, and captured at a specific point in time (e.g., at the time of an order).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Return Policy: a policy that allows customers to return products within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase

entities_post_resolution.name: Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID) | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID) | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master.Cust Id
entities_post_resolution.definition:
CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition:
SALES ORDER
**Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products. | **Business Context**: Orders are created when customers complete checkout. They track the customer, items, payment status, and delivery information. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID

  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer data including identifiers, contact details, regional attributes, status, and record creation timestamp for active customers.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, SKU, name, category reference, price, and whether the product is active for sale.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores hierarchical category data for products, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores header information for sales orders, including customer reference, order date, total amount, status, payment confirmation timestamp, delivery timestamp, and record creation timestamp.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores line item details for each order, including the product, quantity, unit price, and computed line amount.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments associated with orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments associated with orders, including where the shipment originated, when it was shipped, the tracking information, and current status.
